parts.text: "Project @JRedisGraph/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @JRedisGraph/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/jedis-4.3.2-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-jedis-4.3.2/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC JavaParser transformation using the template in @/workspace/javaparser-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the JavaParser API documentation located in folder /workspace/javaparser-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @JRedisGraph/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

I'll help you create a generic JavaParser transformation rule to fix the breaking dependency update in the JRedisGraph project. Let me start by examining the project structure and understanding the compilation errors.

First, let me explore the project structure to understand what we're working with:

state.title: workspace/jedis-4.3.2-api.md

parts.text:
Based on my analysis, I need to create a more practical solution. Let me create the generic transformation rule that addresses the core issue. From the API spec and error messages, I can see that:

1. The pipeline interfaces like `BasicRedisPipeline`, `BinaryRedisPipeline`, etc. have been moved or removed
